summary: First end-to-end test of Centsible (Expo web + FastAPI + MongoDB). Backend: 35/35 pytest cases pass after fixing a critical MongoDB duplicate-key bug on 2nd OTP signup (unique+sparse index on `phone`/`email` matched `null` values). Frontend: full user journey works — landing → sign-in → auto-filled OTP → 3-step onboarding → dashboard → FAB add-expense (numpad + category chip + method + save) → transactions (search+filter+edit+delete visible) → analytics (Week/Month/Year toggle, heatmap, finance-term card rendered with real AI content) → chat (user + bot bubbles with LLM reply) → profile (user card, groups nav, export, sign out). No deprecated Expo packages found. No React Native red screens.

minor.issue: FIXED by testing agent: 2nd email signup crashed with DuplicateKeyError because sparse unique indexes on `phone`/`email` still enforce uniqueness for `null`. Removed `null` field on insert for both OTP and Google routes; dropped and let indexes rebuild in DB.

ui_bugs.issue: The 6 OTP input boxes overflow horizontally on a 390px viewport — only the 1st box is fully visible (see /tmp/02_otp.png). Verify still works because devOTP auto-fills, but a real user cannot see boxes 3-6. Consider smaller boxes / responsive width.

Tab labels are truncated to a single character (e.g. 'H...', 'T...', 'A...') on mobile viewport. Either shorten tab labels, increase tab bar height, or reduce font size so full labels ('Home', 'Txns', 'Analytics', 'AI', 'Profile') render.

issues: 6 OTP boxes overflow off-screen on 390px width
Tab labels truncated to first character

action_items: Review OTP box width/layout for narrow viewports so all 6 digits are visible.
Fix bottom tab bar label truncation (shorter labels or larger tab area).

Optional: convert `email_1` / `phone_1` indexes to partialFilterExpression (`{email: {$exists: true}}`) instead of relying on stripped-null docs, for defense-in-depth against future writes that include null.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line ~246-260: original insert included `email: None` / `phone: None` which broke uniqueness constraints. Fixed here; keep this shape going forward.
server.py `_txn_view.date` returns ISO string only when it is a datetime; for older docs with string dates this is fine but noteworthy for consistency.
AI endpoints correctly wrap LLM calls in try/except with graceful fallbacks — good pattern.
server.py is ~825 lines — approaching the 700 line guideline; consider splitting into routers (auth/txn/ai/groups) for maintainability.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (fixed null email/phone insert on OTP verify and Google session routes)
seed_data_creation: Tests use pytest fixtures that create fresh TEST_<uuid>@centsible.dev users and a seeded user with Swiggy/Uber/Amazon transactions per session. No cleanup performed (test data isolated by user_id).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend + FE both green. Remaining polish items are cosmetic: OTP box overflow and bottom-tab label truncation on 390px width. AI endpoints (finance-term, insight, chat) return live LLM content (verified: coherent Compound Interest term with example/tip; personalized savings tip referencing user's ₹250 Food spend). Backend index fix in `db.users` was applied at runtime — indexes will be recreated on next startup without null docs present.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of the 500 on 2nd OTP signup: MongoDB `sparse: true` unique index only skips docs that are MISSING the field, NOT docs whose field is `null`. The original code always wrote `phone: None` for email signups (and vice versa), so the 2nd email user collided on `phone=null`. Reproduction: POST /api/Auth/login twice with different emails → verify each → 2nd verify returns 500 DuplicateKeyError. Mitigation applied: (1) removed the `None` field assignments so the doc omits the unused identifier field entirely; (2) removed the `phone: null` field from the pre-existing user in DB; (3) dropped the old `phone_1` and `email_1` indexes so startup rebuilds them cleanly. All 35 pytest cases now pass.
